# Principles — ENG domain

<!-- Auto-generated by JOS charter sync. DO NOT EDIT. -->
<!-- Generated at: 2026-07-10T00:00:20.248623+00:00 -->

This index lists every JOS-canonical principle declared under the `eng` domain. Source of truth: jordan-operating-system repo.

**Count:** 9

| ID | Title | Stage | Flexibility | Summary |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| [JOS-P13](JOS-P13-prefer-small-reversible-changes.md) | Prefer Small Reversible Changes | adopt | expected | Commit the system to favoring changes that can be undone over changes that cannot. |
| [JOS-P14](JOS-P14-prefer-explicit-boundaries.md) | Prefer Explicit Boundaries | adopt | expected | Commit the system to making component boundaries visible, documented, and governed. |
| [JOS-P15](JOS-P15-prefer-observable-systems.md) | Prefer Observable Systems | adopt | required | Commit the system to ensuring every production system produces enough signal to diagnose its behavior. |
| [JOS-P16](JOS-P16-prefer-automated-verification.md) | Prefer Automated Verification | adopt | expected | Commit the system to automating verification where possible rather than relying on human inspection. |
| [JOS-P17](JOS-P17-prefer-clear-ownership.md) | Prefer Clear Ownership | adopt | required | Commit the system to ensuring every engineering artifact has a known, accountable owner. |
| [JOS-P18](JOS-P18-verification-proportional-to-risk.md) | Verification Proportional to Risk | adopt | expected | Commit the system to scaling verification effort with the blast radius of a change. |
| [JOS-P19](JOS-P19-decision-records-over-institutional-memory.md) | Decision Records Over Institutional Memory | adopt | expected | Commit the system to recording engineering decisions in durable, discoverable form rather than relying on oral tradition. |
| [JOS-P20](JOS-P20-acyclic-dependencies.md) | Acyclic Dependencies | adopt | suggested | Encourage systems to maintain acyclic dependency graphs at every level of abstraction. |
| [JOS-P21](JOS-P21-defined-degradation-modes.md) | Defined Degradation Modes | adopt | expected | Commit the system to designing explicit degraded states rather than allowing uncontrolled failure. |
